name: Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea
name_original: Pantano de Riba-roja – circular desde Riba-roja por Gandesa y Batea
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Riba-Roja D'Ebre traverse a landscape shaped by the Ebro River and its reservoirs, such as the Pantano de Riba-roja. The region features varied terrain, including rolling hills and more challenging ascents, with routes often passing through agricultural areas and small towns. Elevation gains are notable on many routes, indicating a generally undulating to hilly environment suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Riba-Roja D'Ebre The most popular road cycling route is Flix - La Granadella - Bellaguarda - Margalef - La Palma – circular route, a 48.5 miles (78.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 46 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 1,000 meters of elevation gain through diverse rural scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea, a difficult 53.3 miles (85.8 km) path. This route offers extensive views of the Riba-roja reservoir and passes through several towns. Local road cyclists also love the Plaça Major Flix – Riba-roja Reservoir loop from Flix, a 48.3 miles (77.7 km) trail leading through the Ebro river valley and reservoir surroundings, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Riba-Roja D'Ebre is defined by reservoir views, river valleys, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier routes to advanced road bike routes Riba-Roja D'Ebre with significant climbs. The routes in Riba-Roja D'Ebre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 6 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Riba-Roja D'Ebre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of road cycling routes in Riba-Roja D'Ebre?
A: The road cycling routes around Riba-Roja D'Ebre offer a varied terrain, with a significant number of challenging options. Out of 44 routes, 24 are classified as difficult, 18 as moderate, and 2 as easy. This indicates a generally undulating to hilly environment with notable elevation gains.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Riba-Roja D'Ebre?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs, there are a few easier options available. The region offers 2 routes classified as easy, which are suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes for advanced riders?
A: For advanced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966555278/flix-la-granadella-bellaguarda-margalef-la-palma-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flix - La Granadella - Bellaguarda - Margalef - La Palma – circular route&lt;/a&gt; offer over 1,000 meters of elevation gain over 78 km. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966545886/pantano-de-riba-roja-circular-from-riba-roja-through-gandesa-and-batea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea&lt;/a&gt;, covering 85.8 km with over 1,200 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in the Riba-Roja D'Ebre area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Riba-Roja D'Ebre are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14893427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaça Major Flix – Riba-roja Reservoir loop from Flix&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966545886/pantano-de-riba-roja-circular-from-riba-roja-through-gandesa-and-batea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of views and landscapes can I expect on road cycling routes around Riba-Roja D'Ebre?
A: Road cycling routes in Riba-Roja D'Ebre are defined by stunning reservoir views, particularly around the Pantano de Riba-roja, and scenic river valleys along the Ebro. You'll also encounter rolling hills, agricultural areas, and pass through charming small towns, offering diverse rural scenery.

Q: What are some interesting places to see along the road cycling routes in Riba-Roja D'Ebre?
A: While cycling, you can explore various points of interest. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Church Tower of Fayón&lt;/a&gt;, or experiencing the natural beauty of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5134087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5340832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sebes Nature Reserve Boardwalk&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mineta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Riba-Roja D'Ebre?
A: The road cycling experience in Riba-Roja D'Ebre is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse rural scenery, the extensive views of the Riba-roja reservoir, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs.

Q: Is it possible to access road cycling routes in Riba-Roja D'Ebre using public transport?
A: Riba-Roja D'Ebre has a train station, making it accessible by public transport. You can plan your route to start or end near the station, allowing for convenient access to the cycling network without needing a car.

Q: Are there any hidden gem road bike loops in the Riba-Roja D'Ebre area?
A: Many of the routes offer a sense of discovery through less-traveled paths. For a challenging loop that explores the region's varied terrain, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19830612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riba-roja d'Ebre – Riba-roja Reservoir loop from Faió - la Pobla de Massaluca&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 70 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Riba-Roja D'Ebre?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. While summer can be warm, early mornings or late afternoons can still provide enjoyable rides, especially along routes that offer shade or are near the reservoir.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that pass through towns with cafes or amenities?
A: Yes, several routes are designed to pass through or near towns where you can find cafes and other amenities. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966545886/pantano-de-riba-roja-circular-from-riba-roja-through-gandesa-and-batea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea&lt;/a&gt; passes through multiple towns, providing opportunities for breaks and refreshments.

Q: How does road cycling in Riba-Roja D'Ebre compare to nearby Móra d'Ebre?
A: Both Riba-Roja D'Ebre and Móra d'Ebre offer excellent road cycling opportunities along the Ebro River. Riba-Roja D'Ebre is particularly known for its extensive reservoir views and routes with significant elevation changes, often traversing more rugged, hilly terrain. Móra d'Ebre also provides river valley rides and access to different regional landscapes, but Riba-Roja D'Ebre's routes tend to feature more pronounced climbs and panoramic vistas of the Pantano de Riba-roja.
tips: Riba-roja is located on the right bank of the Ebro, on a fluvial terrace. The streets of the old town are narrow and you can also see the old walled town.
On the outskirts of the town of Riba-roja d'Ebre, this viewpoint offers a beautiful panoramic view of the Ebro River as it passes through the Ribera de Ebro, under the Ribarroja reservoir.

The Riba-roja reservoir is located between Aragon and Catalonia. The reservoir has a hydroelectric power plant, which is why its main use is power generation, although it is also used for irrigation.
and water supply to the towns in the area.
